title: CEL Policy Evaluation Middleware Config
description:
Configuration for the CEL policy evaluation middleware plugin. Evaluates inline CEL expressions against request context.

Two modes:
- **Access-control** (default, no `on_match`): `true` â†’ continue, `false` â†’ 403 Forbidden.
- **`on_match`** (present): on `true`, take the configured actions â€” write context keys (`set_context`) and/or reject with a configured status + code (`deny`). On `false`, continue unchanged (no 403). When both `set_context` and `deny` are present, `deny` wins on a match â€” a denied request is not also written to context.

expression.description: CEL expression that must evaluate to a boolean. Has access to a `request` object with fields: `method`, `path`, `query`, `headers`, `body`, `client_ip`, `path_params`, `consumer`, `claims`.

deny_message.description: Custom message returned in the 403 Forbidden response body when the expression evaluates to false (access-control mode only; ignored when `on_match` is set).

on_match.description: Actions to take when the expression evaluates to `true`. On a `false` result the request continues unchanged (no 403). Stack multiple `cel` instances to compose multiple match rules. When both `set_context` and `deny` are configured, a matched request is denied without context being written.

set_context.description: Key-value pairs written into the request context when the expression is true. The `ai-proxy` dispatcher reads `ai.target` to select the active provider target.

deny.description: Reject the request with the given problem+json status and code when the expression is true. The `code` becomes the URN suffix on the `type` field and is exposed as the `code` field on the response body â€” the convention used by `ai-proxy` for `model_not_permitted` and similar.

status.description: HTTP status code. Must be 4xx â€” denying as 5xx would mask a policy decision as a server fault. Out-of-range values fall back to 403.

code.description: Machine-readable error code. snake_case to match the `error.type` convention used by `ai-proxy` and OpenAI-style APIs (e.g. `model_not_permitted`, `invalid_request_error`). Becomes the URN suffix on the response `type` field.

message.description: Human-readable detail message. Falls back to `code` when omitted.
